notes: Staged LLM extraction directly from each source span. Consumes only the span text and declared source metadata; never the manual object set or expected_* labels. Review priority is assigned application-side by type.

predictions.predicted_statement: New clients must pay the standard deposit before scheduling can be finalized unless an approved exception exists.
After‑hours scheduling normally requires operations lead approval, except for emergency priority incidents.
When active water damage, loss of heat, or another safety issue is present, dispatch may treat it as an emergency and bypass the normal after‑hours approval step.
VIP maintenance clients may bypass the standard deposit when the dispatcher confirms the account and records the reason.
Before confirming the job, check for a documented exception and record the waiver reason in the job record.
Emergency dispatch must classify the incident and verify technician availability.
Long-term maintenance clients are identified by an active maintenance plan and at least one service visit in the past year.
The VIP client list is an internal roster of long‑term maintenance accounts that receive deposit flexibility and expedited handling.
Emergency priority will apply only to active damage, safety hazards, or service outages that affect core habitability.

predictions.predicted_title: Notify field lead when tech unavailable
predictions.predicted_statement: The dispatcher must inform the field lead if no qualified technician is available for a same‑day emergency request.
All deposit exceptions must be recorded in the job record prior to confirming scheduling.
